element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary melee option, likely introduced in opening levels to establish Lo Wang's martial identity
Japanese-themed ranged weapon, thematic to protagonist
Standard dual-wield firearms for sustained damage
Powerful late-game weapon with iconic one-liner trigger ('Time to get erased!')
First FPS appearance of sticky bomb mechanic; requires tactical placement
Vertical navigation mechanic; establishes 3D environmental interaction
Vehicle combat sequences; adds pacing variety
Stationary weapon emplacement for area control
Non-combat progression gating; interleaved with combat encounters

typical_relief_triggers: Safe zone traversal between combat arenas
Vehicle piloting sequences (shift to different gameplay mode)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating combat encounter clusters with puzzle-gated progression; vehicle sequences break rhythm; early game emphasizes melee and basic gunplay, mid-to-late game escalates to exotic weapons and coordinated multi-enemy scenarios

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Vertical exploration via climbable ladders; room-over-room architecture enables hidden areas and non-linear level traversal
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Multi-level vertical space encourages vertical exploration; Build engine room-over-room technical innovation suggests secreted content in overhead/vertical spaces

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of katana and basic firearms (Uzis); simple enemy encounters; puzzle-focused progression gating; teaches core movement and combat mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation to riot gun and sticky bombs; multi-enemy encounters; introduction of vehicle sequences and turret placement; puzzle complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Railgun and enemy-part weaponization; dense enemy clusters; Master Zilla battle in piloted samurai-styled war mech; cumulative mastery of all mechanics required
notable_spikes: First multi-enemy encounter (tests basic combat proficiency)
Vehicle sequences (requires adaptation to different control scheme)
Final Master Zilla mech boss battle (capstone challenge combining movement, weapon switching, and environmental awareness)

notable_level_patterns.description: Build engine innovation enabling simultaneous rendering of stacked interior spaces; creates opportunities for non-linear navigation, secret areas via vertical exploration, and layered combat encounters
Puzzle solution required to unlock progression to next combat encounter; establishes rhythm of cognitive break between action sequences
Mid-game insertion of drivable tank/armed vehicle sequences; shifts gameplay from FPS exploration to vehicle combat; provides pacing variety
Railgun pickup triggers iconic Lo Wang one-liner ('Time to get erased!'); thematic weapon design (katana, shurikens) reinforces Japanese setting and protagonist identity
